mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on future cash flows declines and equity risk premia compress, pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is also easing, because a softer inflation backdrop reduces the odds of renewed tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive on average after monthly declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and slower demand growth, which leads analysts to mark down earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative average 20-day returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 55% of trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broadening nominal demand and labor income growth, which supports revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive when both are above their prior 3-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO after a soft patch indicates improving industrial output and inventory rebuilding, which typically supports cyclical earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if rebounds in INDPRO are not followed by above-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign flips negative in more than half of rebound cases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, policy remains restrictive and inflation-adjusted financing costs stay high, which compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL trend is flat to rising over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes with firm inflation do not produce negative 20-day returns, or if the combined condition has no edge versus a FEDFUNDS-only trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS signals payroll weakness and slower disposable income growth, which reduces near-term consumption expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over one week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines are not followed by negative 5-day returns more often than chance, or if the average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad real and nominal demand strength, which raises earnings growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales strength does not predict positive 60-day returns, or if the interaction term is not materially stronger than either component alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing recovery, rising INDPRO together with rising HOUST signals cyclical production and construction demand, which improves earnings breadth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent gains in industrial production and housing starts do not predict positive 20-day returns, or if the effect disappears when housing weakens.
